message:
chore: sync concurrent agent changes (app, bench_budget, beads)

- app.rs: Minor updates from concurrent development
- bench_budget.sh: Enhanced benchmark script
- beads: Issue tracker state sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1un","title":"Document Terminal Compatibility Matrix","description":"# Terminal Compatibility Matrix Documentation

## Background
Plan Chapter 16 provides a detailed compatibility matrix. This needs to be documented for users to understand what features work where.

## Compatibility Matrix (from Plan)

| Feature | Kitty | WezTerm | Alacritty | Ghostty | iTerm2 | GNOME Term | Win Term |
|---------|-------|---------|-----------|---------|--------|------------|----------|
| True Color | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Sync Output | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Kitty Keyboard | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Kitty Graphics | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Sixel | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Focus Events | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Bracketed Paste | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

## Feature Details

### True Color (24-bit RGB)
- Detection: COLORTERM=truecolor or COLORTERM=24bit
- Fallback: 256-color or 16-color palette
- Most modern terminals support this

### Synchronized Output (DEC 2026)
- Purpose: Atomic frame updates, flicker prevention
- Detection: Known terminal identification
- Fallback: Buffered writes still help
- ftui activates when detected

### OSC 8 Hyperlinks
- Purpose: Clickable links in terminal
- Format: `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\text\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`
- Windows Terminal: Not supported
- Most Linux/macOS terminals support

### Kitty Keyboard Protocol
- Purpose: Enhanced key event information
- Provides: press/repeat/release, modifiers
- Limited adoption outside Kitty ecosystem
- Feature-gated in ftui

### Scroll Region (DECSTBM)
- Purpose: Inline mode optimization
- Support: Nearly universal
- Quirks: tmux/screen passthrough issues
- ftui uses as optimization, not requirement

## Multiplexer Notes

### tmux
- Passthrough: Requires `\x1bPtmux;...\x1b\\`
- Sync output: Blocked by default
- OSC 8: Needs recent version + config
- Detection: TMUX env var

### screen
- Limited modern feature support
- No sync output passthrough
- Basic ANSI works fine
- Detection: STY env var

### Zellij
- Better passthrough than tmux/screen
- Native terminal support varies
- Detection: ZELLIJ env var

## Windows Considerations

### Windows Terminal
- True color: ✓
- Mouse: ✓
- OSC 8: ✗
- Sync output: ✗
- Raw mode: Via Crossterm

### ConEmu/Cmder
- Limited feature set
- True color: ✓ (newer versions)
- ANSI support varies by version

### cmd.exe / PowerShell (legacy)
- Very limited ANSI support
- Windows 10+ improved support
- Use Windows Terminal for best experience

## Inline Mode Notes
- Works everywhere (no special requirements)
- Cursor save/restore: Universal
- Scroll region: Nearly universal (optimization only)
- Sync output: Nice-to-have (not required)

## Recommended Test Matrix
For CI, test against:
1. Kitty (most features)
2. WezTerm (cross-platform)
3. Alacritty (popular, minimal)
4. Plain Linux VT (baseline)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Matrix table documented
- [ ] Feature descriptions complete
- [ ] Multiplexer notes included
- [ ] Windows section clear
- [ ] Inline mode notes present
- [ ] Test matrix recommended

 {"id":"bd-1xo","title":"Windows Terminal compatibility layer","description":"PHASE 7 - POLISH: WINDOWS SUPPORT

## Background
Windows Terminal v1 support is deferred but must have clear integration path. Modern Windows Terminal supports ANSI, older conhost needs different handling.

## Technical Requirements (v1 Scope - Windows Terminal Only)
- Detect Windows Terminal vs legacy conhost
- Enable VT processing mode via SetConsoleMode
- Handle Windows-specific escape sequences
- PTY spawning via ConPTY API
- Signal handling differences (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+Break)
- Path handling for Windows conventions

## Out of Scope for v1
- Legacy conhost support (Windows 7/8)
- PowerShell-specific handling
- Windows-specific widgets

## Design Rationale
Per ADR-004, Windows v1 targets modern Windows Terminal only. This dramatically simplifies implementation while covering majority of Windows developer use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Windows Terminal detection
- [ ] VT mode enabling
- [ ] ConPTY integration for PTY tests
- [ ] Signal handling shim
- [ ] CI testing on Windows
- [ ] Feature gate: windows

## Testing
- GitHub Actions Windows runner
- Manual testing on Windows Terminal
- Document known limitations

 {"id":"bd-1yvn","title":"Integrate budget checking into frame loop","description":"# Integrate budget checking into frame loop

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). The frame loop must create budgets, check them during rendering, and handle exhaustion.

## Technical Design

```rust
impl<M: Model> Program<M> {
    pub fn run_frame(&mut self, terminal: &mut Terminal) -> io::Result<()> {
        // Create budget for this frame
        let mut budget = RenderBudget::new(self.config.budget.total);
        
        // Track consecutive overruns for degradation cooldown
        static OVERRUN_COUNT: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
        static STABLE_COUNT: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
        
        // Render phase with budget
        let frame = self.render_with_budget(&mut budget);
        
        // Check for degradation need
        if budget.should_degrade(self.config.budget.cost_estimates.unicode_border) {
            if OVERRUN_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed) >= self.config.budget.degradation_cooldown {
                budget.degrade();
                OVERRUN_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
                STABLE_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
            }
        } else {
            STABLE_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
        
        // Check for upgrade opportunity
        if budget.should_upgrade() && STABLE_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed) >= self.config.budget.upgrade_threshold {
            budget.upgrade();
            STABLE_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
        
        // Present phase
        if !budget.exhausted() {
            self.presenter.present_with_budget(frame, &budget)?;
        } else if self.config.budget.log_overruns {
            tracing::warn!(
                elapsed_ms = budget.elapsed().as_millis(),
                degradation = ?budget.degradation(),
                "Frame budget exhausted, skipping present"
            );
        }
        
        // Record metrics
        self.metrics.record_frame(budget.elapsed(), budget.degradation());
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn render_with_budget(&mut self, budget: &mut RenderBudget) -> Frame {
        let mut frame = Frame::new(self.terminal_size);
        
        // Pass budget to view so widgets can check it
        let model_view = self.model.view(budget);
        
        // Render widget tree with budget awareness
        model_view.render(frame.area(), &mut frame.buffer, budget);
        
        frame
    }
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
1. **Cooldown prevents oscillation**: Dont degrade on single overrun
2. **Upgrade requires stability**: Must be under budget for N frames
3. **Frame skip is last resort**: Only when allow_frame_skip enabled
4. **Metrics recorded**: For observability and tuning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Frame loop creates RenderBudget per frame
- [ ] Budget passed to render and present phases
- [ ] Cooldown logic prevents oscillation
- [ ] Upgrade logic requires stable performance
- [ ] Frame skip when exhausted
- [ ] Metrics recorded for every frame","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:21.317947151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:56:38.986031743Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:56:38.985962553Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yvn","depends_on_id":"bd-389n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:17:22.583062667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1yvn","depends_on_id":"bd-3tfu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:17:21.444384239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1z02","title":"Optional OpenTelemetry Audit Trail","description":"Add optional OTEL plumbing (via tracing) so apps can export runtime spans/decisions to a collector without default performance cost.

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Optional OpenTelemetry Audit Trail
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Epic includes a perf budget + regression suite (hyperfine + flamegraphs + CI gates) with explainable failures


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Drift/Regimes: if runtime metrics vary (resize storms, frame pacing), incorporate change-point detection and regime-specific policies.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
- Add JSONL perf logs with schema: run_id, case, env, features, seed, metrics, budgets, pass/fail, and artifact paths.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:14:12.856214035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:58:18.165110792Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","performance","telemetry","tracing"],"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-1z02","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- User feedback asks for an optional, high‑level audit trail of agent decisions and runtime spans, exported to an OTEL collector.
- Requirement: performance‑oriented tracing is not the goal; the desired value is observability and auditability.

Goals
- Provide opt‑in OTEL export via env vars (e.g., OTEL_HTTP_ENDPOINT, OTEL_TRACE_ID, OTEL_PARENT_SPAN_ID).
- Ensure zero overhead by default: feature‑gated dependencies, and no initialization unless env is set.
- Allow consumers to attach FrankenTUI spans to an existing trace if IDs are provided.

Non‑Goals
- Do not force a global tracing subscriber on library users.
- Do not introduce blocking network calls in the hot path.

Risks / Considerations
- Tracing subscriber interactions can conflict with app‑level tracing if not carefully scoped.
- OTEL exporters can be heavy; must be optional and well‑documented.

Deliverables
- Architecture and API spec for telemetry initialization.
- Implementation with feature flags and tests.
- Docs explaining env vars and how to integrate into host apps.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:14:20Z"},{"id":146,"issue_id":"bd-1z02","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan Update
- Added subscriber integration strategy, trace context parsing utility, and OTEL E2E test harness with verbose logging for high-confidence validation.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:20:40Z"},{"id":150,"issue_id":"bd-1z02","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan Update
- Added explicit audit event schema + redaction policy, plus telemetry performance budgets and microbench guardrails.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:55Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1z02","title":"Optional OpenTelemetry Audit Trail","description":"Add optional OTEL plumbing (via tracing) so apps can export runtime spans/decisions to a collector without default performance cost.

## Success Criteria

- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests

- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.

 {"id":"bd-1z02.1","title":"OTEL + tracing research (Rust 2024 best practices)","description":"Survey current tracing/opentelemetry integration patterns, env var conventions, and context propagation approaches.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: OTEL + tracing research (Rust 2024 best practices)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:14:25.391200148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:34:14.553627698Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:34:14.553464411Z","close_reason":"Research summary posted (crates/versions, env var contract, integration pattern)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["research","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z02.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1z02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:14:25.391200148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":110,"issue_id":"bd-1z02.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Research Questions
- Current recommended crates: `tracing-opentelemetry`, `opentelemetry`, `opentelemetry-otlp`, `opentelemetry_sdk`.
- HTTP exporter configuration for OTLP, including env var usage and defaults.
- How to set parent context using explicit trace/span IDs (if supported) vs W3C traceparent.
- Best practices for keeping cost low when exporter is disabled.

Deliverable
- A short writeup of recommended integration patterns and a list of crates/versions to use.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:14:31Z"},{"id":240,"issue_id":"bd-1z02.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Research summary (2026-02-03):\n\nRecommended crates/versions (from opentelemetry-rust 0.31.0 release line):\n- opentelemetry = "0.31"\n- opentelemetry_sdk = "0.31"\n- opentelemetry-otlp = "0.31"\n- tracing-opentelemetry = "0.32.1" (note: tracing-opentelemetry versions are not synced; typically one ahead of opentelemetry crates)\n- tracing = "0.1", tracing-subscriber = "0.3"\n\nIntegration pattern:\n1) Build SdkTracerProvider with OTLP exporter; prefer batch processor for production. SimpleSpanProcessor is synchronous (debug). BatchSpanProcessor uses background thread and is higher throughput.\n2) Install tracer provider and a tracing subscriber with OpenTelemetryLayer (tracing-opentelemetry).\n3) Configure global propagator (TraceContext + Baggage) via TextMapCompositePropagator, or use OTEL_PROPAGATORS env var.\n4) For explicit parent context, use OpenTelemetrySpanExt::set_parent and Context extraction/injection.\n\nEnv var contract to support (aligned to OTel spec):\n- OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true => no-op SDK.\n- OTEL_SERVICE_NAME, OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES for Resource (service.name).\n- OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER / OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER / OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER (default otlp).\n- OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT (default gRPC 4317, HTTP 4318; HTTP appends /v1/traces etc).\n- OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL (+ *_TRACES_PROTOCOL, *_METRICS_PROTOCOL) values: grpc | http/protobuf | http/json.\n- OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS (+ per-signal) key=value list.\n\nNote: opentelemetry-otlp 0.31 notes env vars do not override code config; if we want env-driven config, avoid hard-coded exporter settings.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:33:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z02.10","title":"Audit event schema + redaction policy","description":"Define the high-level event schema for telemetry (decision events, spans) and a redaction policy for sensitive data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Audit event schema + redaction policy
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:30.882536072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:08:09.461981111Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:08:09.461912212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","privacy","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z02.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1z02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:30.882536072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1z02.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1z02.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:47.982993374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":148,"issue_id":"bd-1z02.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Define event names and fields for decisions, transitions, and runtime phases.
- Define redaction rules (what never leaves the process by default).
- Determine how user-supplied fields are handled or dropped.

Acceptance Criteria
- Schema is explicit and stable; consumers can build dashboards.
- Redaction is conservative by default.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z02.11","title":"Telemetry performance budget + microbench","description":"Define performance targets for telemetry (disabled and enabled) and add microbench/measurements to guard regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Telemetry performance budget + microbench
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:39.637064608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:45:00.074299345Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:45:00.074272906Z","close_reason":"Added telemetry perf JSONL test, budgets, hyperfine baseline, flamegraph attempt","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","performance","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z02.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1z02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:39.637064608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1z02.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1z02.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:48.421366413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1z02.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1z02.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:48.562281301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-1z02.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Bench Targets
- Disabled path: near-zero overhead (baseline vs no-telemetry build).
- Enabled path: bounded overhead for span emission and export batching.

Acceptance Criteria
- Concrete numeric budget documented.
- Microbench results logged; regressions flagged.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:22:44Z"}]}
@@ -828,7 +828,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vins","title":"Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes","description":"## Background
The typewriter effect is limited to left-to-right reveal. A comprehensive reveal system enables dramatic text appearances for titles, announcements, and onboarding.

## Implementation

Add RevealMode enum (used as parameter, not nested in TextEffect):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum RevealMode {
    #[default]
    LeftToRight,    // Classic typewriter
    RightToLeft,    // Reverse typewriter  
    CenterOut,      // Expand from middle
    EdgesIn,        // Converge to center
    Random,         // Random order (deterministic with seed)
    ByWord,         // Word at a time
    ByLine,         // Line at a time (for multi-line)
}
```

Add TextEffect variant (consistent structure with others):
```rust
TextEffect::Reveal {
    mode: RevealMode,
    progress: f64,      // 0.0 = hidden, 1.0 = fully revealed
    seed: u64,          // For Random mode determinism
}

// Separate variant for gradient mask (different parameters)
TextEffect::RevealMask {
    angle: f64,         // Sweep angle in degrees (0 = left-to-right)
    progress: f64,      // Mask position 0.0-1.0
    softness: f64,      // Edge softness 0.0 (hard) to 1.0 (gradient)
}
```

### Visibility Calculation by Mode:

| Mode | Visible When |
|------|--------------|
| LeftToRight | char_idx < progress * total_chars |
| RightToLeft | char_idx >= (1-progress) * total_chars |
| CenterOut | distance_from_center < progress * max_distance |
| EdgesIn | distance_from_edge < progress * max_distance |
| Random | hash(seed, idx) < progress * total_chars |
| ByWord | word_idx < progress * total_words |
| ByLine | line_idx < progress * total_lines |
| RevealMask | rotated_position > threshold (with softness fade) |

### Usage:
```rust
// Simple left-to-right reveal
StyledText::new("Hello World")
    .effect(TextEffect::Reveal { 
        mode: RevealMode::LeftToRight, 
        progress: 0.5,
        seed: 0,
    })

// Dramatic gradient mask sweep
StyledText::new("WELCOME")
    .effect(TextEffect::RevealMask {
        angle: 45.0,
        progress: 0.5,
        softness: 0.3,
    })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reveal_ltr_first_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows first half
- test_reveal_rtl_last_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows last half
- test_reveal_center_out_middle_first: Center chars visible first
- test_reveal_edges_in_edges_first: Edge chars visible first
- test_reveal_random_deterministic: Same seed = same reveal order
- test_reveal_random_all_chars_reveal: progress=1 shows all
- test_reveal_by_word_whole_words: Partial progress shows complete words
- test_reveal_by_line_whole_lines: Lines reveal atomically
- test_reveal_mask_angle_0: Left-to-right sweep
- test_reveal_mask_angle_90: Top-to-bottom sweep  
- test_reveal_mask_softness_0: Hard edge (binary visible/hidden)
- test_reveal_mask_softness_1: Gradient fade across chars

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot CenterOut at progress 0.25, 0.5, 0.75
- Snapshot RevealMask at angle=45, softness=0.3

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-vu0o","title":"Demo Showcase: Theme Studio / Live Palette Editor","description":"## Background

Themes are the fastest way to make a TUI feel "impossibly premium". FrankenTUI already has:
- a semantic token system (fg/bg/accent/status/priority)
- alpha-aware overlays
- WCAG-aware contrast helpers

This screen turns the theme engine into an interactive instrument:
- inspect presets
- edit palette values live
- validate contrast
- export for real terminal emulators

## UX / Core Interactions

- Preset list (Cyberpunk Aurora, Darcula, Lumen Light, Nordic Frost, plus any future presets).
- Token inspector:
  - show each semantic token swatch
  - show computed contrast ratio for fg/bg pairs
- Live editor:
  - adjust hue/chroma/lightness or RGB
  - lock constraints (e.g., "keep WCAG AA")
  - preview immediately across the app

Suggested keybindings:
- Ctrl+T: cycle presets (global convention)
- Enter: edit selected token
- Esc: cancel edit
- E: export

## Export Targets (Impressive + Practical)

- Export current theme to:
  - FrankenTUI theme JSON (for reproducible bug reports)
  - Ghostty theme config snippet (user-visible win)
  - optional: other formats later

## Architecture Notes

- Core theme logic must live in library code (ftui-style / ftui-extras::theme), not in the demo.
- Theme Studio is a UI over that API.
- Cache derived semantic styles (semantic swatches) per theme to keep toggles O(1).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Live updates are immediate (no restart) and deterministic.
- Contrast validation is shown in the UI and enforced in tests.
- Export produces stable output with a versioned schema.

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Constraint-based palette editing:
  - treat "edit a token" as a constrained optimization problem in perceptual space (OKLCH)
  - enforce minimum contrast constraints while minimizing perceptual delta from the user's intent
  - surface a tiny evidence ledger when the solver adjusts the requested color

## Extreme Optimization

- Theme switching and editing must not allocate on the hot path.
- Add a toggle-latency regression test (bd-vu0o.2).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:46.962183681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:26:28.171702525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","theme","ux"],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-vu0o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Live editing of theme tokens with immediate UI preview.
- Toggle between presets and custom adjustments.

Testing
- Unit tests for theme update propagation and serialization.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logging for palette changes.
